text.title: CSS-in-JS Antipatterns
text.summary: Mistakes that cause class explosion, RSC breakage, untyped tokens, or hydration failures.

antipattern.description: Defining styled.div or styled.button inside a function component body.
antipattern.reason: A new class name is generated on every render call. This causes class explosion in the stylesheet, React DevTools shows hundreds of one-time classes, and the browser must repeatedly re-compute styles. Define styled components at module scope always.

antipattern.description: Using runtime CSS-in-JS (styled-components, @emotion/styled) in a Next.js App Router Server Component without 'use client' directive.
antipattern.reason: Runtime CSS-in-JS requires the browser's JS context to generate styles. Server Components have no client context. The build fails or produces hydration mismatches that are hard to diagnose. Use zero-runtime alternatives or add 'use client' to the component.

antipattern.description: Passing $variant or $size props without the $ prefix: styled.button with variant="primary" on the element.
antipattern.reason: Unknown props are forwarded to the DOM element, triggering React warnings in development and potentially invalid HTML. styled-components strips transient ($-prefixed) props before DOM forwarding.

antipattern.description: Skipping the styled.d.ts TypeScript augmentation for DefaultTheme.
antipattern.reason: Without augmentation, all theme property access is typed as any. Token name typos go undetected until runtime. Renaming a token does not trigger TypeScript errors, and the bug surfaces only as an undefined style value.

antipattern.description: Installing both styled-components and @emotion/styled in the same project.
antipattern.reason: Both export a css helper and a styled factory. Mixed imports at different files cause the wrong css function to be called, producing styles that do not compile or produce unexpected output. Two runtime engines also double the JS overhead.

antipattern.description: Hardcoding color literals inside styled templates: background: #3b82f6 instead of ${({ theme }) => theme.colors.primary}.
antipattern.reason: Hardcoded colors bypass the token system. Theme changes, dark mode, and brand updates require grep-and-replace across all components instead of a single token update.

antipattern.description: Skipping SSR style extraction in frameworks with server rendering (Next.js Pages Router, Gatsby).
antipattern.reason: Without extracting critical CSS server-side, the HTML arrives with no inline styles. The browser renders an unstyled page first, then re-renders after hydration — a visible Flash Of Unstyled Content (FOUC) on every first page load.
